data.title: ResMed beats quarterly profit estimates on strong demand for its sleep devices
data.description:
Medical device maker ResMed on Thursday beat Wall Street estimates for second-quarter profit on the back of strong demand for its devices used to manage sleep apnea.

The company makes a non-invasive treatment known as continuous positive airway pressure, or CPAP, machine that helps manage sleep apnea - a common disorder characterized by brief interruptions of breathing during sleep.

Some analysts had expected the U.S. approval of Eli Lilly's blockbuster drug, Zepbound, to treat sleep apnea would reduce demand for the company's devices.

However, ResMed said it expects consumer wearables that track sleep health and GLP-1 therapies such as Zepbound to encourage more patients to use its devices.

"As we move into the second half of fiscal year 2026, we will continue to invest in innovation to scale our digital health capabilities and expand global access to life-saving care, while delivering sustainable, profitable growth," CEO Mick Farrell said.

ResMed's second-quarter revenue rose 11% to $1.42 billion from a year ago, slightly beating analysts' average estimates of $1.40 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company posted adjusted profit of $2.81 per share for the quarter ended December 31, compared with estimates of $2.72 per share.

Last quarter, ResMed reported a $16 million restructuring charge related to company-wide workforce planning initiatives.

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq finish lower as Microsoft, tech weigh
data.description:
Key points:
  • Nasdaq, S&P 500 end lower; Dow up slightly
  • Tech weakest S&P 500 sector; Comm Svcs leads gainers
  • Dollar ~flat; gold up slightly; crude rallies >3%; bitcoin falls >5.5%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield edges down to ~4.24%

S&P 500, NASDAQ FINISH LOWER AS MICROSOFT, TECH WEIGH

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended lower on Thursday, with shares of Microsoft finishing with a 10% decline amid worries following its quarterly report and with the technology sector weighing the most on the S&P 500. The Dow edged higher.

Hefty outlays behind Microsoft's OpenAI alliance increased investor worries about whether artificial intelligence spending will work out for companies. Companies and their AI interests have been behind big gains in the market in recent years.

Other software stocks also fell, while shares of Tesla ended down 3.5%, also in the wake of its quarterly report, and Meta Platforms gained 10.4%. Meta's revenue growth suggested that its AI pivot was paying off.

Year-over-year S&P 500 companies' earnings growth for the fourth quarter of 2025 is now estimated at 10.2%, based on results from 133 of the S&P 500 companies and estimates for the rest, according to LSEG data Thursday. That is up from 9.2% on Friday.

Concerns about a possible U.S. military attack on Iran pushed up oil prices along with energy shares The energy index rose 1.1% and is leading year-to-date gains among the major S&P 500 sectors.

Energy companies Exxon Mobil and Chevron are both due to report quarterly results on Friday.

Here is a snapshot of where markets stood shortly after 4 p.m. ET:

data.title: Western Digital 2Q Profit Rises as AI Boosts Demand
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Western Digital logged higher profit and sales in its latest quarter, boosted by the growth of artificial intelligence.

The data storage company on Thursday posted a fiscal second-quarter profit of $1.84 billion, or $4.73 a share, compared with $594 million, or $1.27 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were $2.13, ahead of estimates of $1.93 a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue climbed 25% to $3.02 billion, topping analyst estimates of $2.93 billion.

The results reflect the company's efforts to meet demand in a market driven by AI, Chief Executive Irving Tan said.

Looking ahead, Western Digital expects its revenue growth to continue and profitability to improve due to continued data center demand and adoption of its high-capacity drives, Finance Chief Kris Sennesael said.

For the current quarter, Western Digital forecast revenue of $3.2 billion, plus or minus $100 million, and adjusted per share earnings of $2.30, give or take 15 cents. Analysts were looking for revenue of $2.98 billion and adjusted per-share earnings of $1.99.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Nelnet to Announce Fourth Quarter Results; Board Approves Dividend
data.description:
LINCOLN, Neb., Jan. 29, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Nelnet, Inc. today announced the Nelnet Board of Directors declared a first quarter cash dividend on the company's outstanding shares of Class A common stock and Class B common stock of $0.33 per share. The dividend will be paid on Friday, March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business on Friday, February 27, 2026.

The company also announced it will release earnings for the fourth quarter and year ended December 31, 2025, after the close of the New York Stock Exchange on Thursday, February 26, 2026. Upon release, additional earnings information will be available at www.nelnetinvestors.com.

Learn more about Nelnet at www.nelnetinc.com.

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/nelnet-to-announce-fourth-quarter-results-board-approves-dividend-302674518.html

SOURCE Nelnet, Inc.

data.title: Australia's Origin Energy posts 10% sequential rise in Q2 APLNG revenue
data.description:
Australia's Origin Energy on Friday posted a 10% sequential rise in revenue from its stake in the Australia Pacific LNG (APLNG) project in the fiscal second quarter, helped by higher LNG sales.

The power producer said revenue from its stake in APLNG - a joint venture with ConocoPhillips and Sinopec - was A$528 million ($372.13 million) for the three months ended December 31, compared with A$482 million in the September quarter.

($1 = 1.4188 Australian dollars)

data.title: EAGLE MATERIALS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Eagle Materials Inc., a leading provider of building materials, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting the challenges and opportunities faced in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: $555.96 million, a decrease of $2.0 million compared to the same period last year, primarily due to lower sales volume in the Gypsum Wallboard segment.

Gross Profit: $160.91 million, a 10% decrease attributed to lower gross sales prices and increased operating costs.

Earnings Before Income Taxes: $129.25 million, a 16% decline driven by lower Gross Profit and higher Corporate General and Administrative expenses.

Net Earnings: $102.90 million, a 14% decrease compared to the same period last year, impacted by lower gross sales prices and higher operating costs.

Diluted Earnings per Share (EPS): $3.22, a 10% decrease reflecting the decline in Net Earnings.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

  • Heavy Materials Sector: Cement revenue increased by 9% for the three months ended December 31, 2025, driven by higher sales volume. Concrete and Aggregates revenue increased by 21% due to higher sales volumes and prices.

Sales Units

  • Cement Sales Volume: Increased by 9% to 1.861 million tons for the three months ended December 31, 2025.
  • Concrete Sales Volume: Remained stable at 298 thousand cubic yards.
  • Aggregates Sales Volume: Increased significantly by 81% to 1.612 million tons.

Geographical Performance

  • The company operates in the U.S. heartland, with cement operations primarily in California, Colorado, Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Missouri, Nebraska, Nevada, Ohio, Oklahoma, Tennessee, and Texas.
  • Significant increases in aggregates sales volume were observed in the Northern Colorado and Northern Kentucky markets.

New Production Launches

  • The company completed the acquisition of Bullskin Stone & Lime LLC in Western Pennsylvania, expanding its aggregates business. This acquisition is expected to enhance the company's presence in the region and contribute to future revenue growth.

Future Outlook

  • The outlook for cement demand remains favorable due to ongoing infrastructure projects funded by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act and strong state Department of Transportation budgets.
  • The residential construction market faces challenges due to high mortgage rates and economic uncertainties, impacting gypsum wallboard sales.

Cost Management

  • The company is managing its cost structure effectively, with substantial raw material reserves supporting its low-cost producer position.
  • Energy costs have decreased slightly and are expected to remain stable, while freight costs have increased but are expected to stabilize.

SEC Filing: EAGLE MATERIALS INC [ EXP ] - 10-Q - Jan. 29, 2026

data.title: Apple Delivers On Cook’s Promise With Record Q1 Revenue, iPhone Sales
data.description:
Apple Inc. (AAPL) reported its first-quarter (Q1) 2026 results after-hours on Thursday, beating street expectations for revenue and earnings, on strong iPhone and Services sales.

The iPhone maker posted revenue of $143.8 billion, 16% higher than $124.3 billion from the same period last year. The latest revenue numbers beat street expectations of $138.22 billion as per data on Fiscal.ai based on 31 analyst estimates.

Earnings also beat market estimates, with Q1 earnings per share coming in at $2.84, 19% higher year-on-year compared to $2.4 last year, and above analyst estimates of $2.66, as per Fiscal.ai data.

“iPhone had its best-ever quarter driven by unprecedented demand, with all-time records across every geographic segment, and Services also achieved an all-time revenue record, up 14 percent from a year ago,” said CEO Tim Cook.

“We are also excited to announce that our installed base now has more than 2.5 billion active devices, which is a testament to incredible customer satisfaction for the very best products and services in the world,” he added.

During Apple’s fourth-quarter earnings call, Cook said the company expected its December quarter to be its best on record.

Shares of AAPL surged over 1.35% after-hours at the time of writing.

Get updates to this developing story directly on Stocktwits.

data.title: Origin Energy Tightens Integrated Gas Production Guidance
data.description:
(21:31 GMT) *Origin Energy 2Q Integrated Gas Revenue A$2.10 Billion, Steady on 1Q
(21:32 GMT) *Origin Energy 2Q Integrated Gas Production 169.0 PJ, Steady on 1Q
(21:32 GMT) *Origin Energy Narrows FY 2026 Production Guidance for Integrated Gas
(21:32 GMT) *Origin Energy Expects FY 2026 Integrated Gas Production of 645-680 PJ
(21:33 GMT) *Origin Energy Says FY 2026 Integrated Gas Capex, Opex Guidance Unchanged
(21:33 GMT) *Origin Energy Received Dividends of A$542 Million for Integrated Gas in 1H
(21:34 GMT) *Origin Energy Expects Dividends of A$700 Million-A$950 Million for Integrated Gas in FY 2026
(21:34 GMT) *Origin Energy Says Electricity Sales Volumes in Energy Markets Fell by 3% in 2Q
(21:35 GMT) *Origin Energy Says Gas Volumes in Energy Markets Fell by 6% in 2Q
(21:35 GMT) *Origin Energy Says FY 2026 Anticipate Coal Volumes Contracted or Hedged at Lower Prices Than FY 2025
(21:42 GMT) Origin Energy Tightens Integrated Gas Production Guidance

By David Winning

SYDNEY--Origin Energy narrowed guidance for annual production by its Integrated Gas business after a second-quarter performance that largely mirrored what in achieved in the opening quarter of its fiscal year.

Origin said on Friday that it now expects between 645 and 680 petajoules of natural gas in the 12 months through June. That represented a tighter range than earlier guidance of 635 and 680 petajoules.

Origin's Integrated Gas business encompasses its share of the Australia Pacific Liquefied Natural Gas export facility in Australia's Queensland state.

"Continued focus on operational performance at Australia Pacific LNG has delivered steady production and revenue, and supported reliable gas supply for export and domestic customers," said Chief Executive Frank Calabria.

Origin said the Integrated Gas business produced 169.0 petajoules in the three months through December, steady on the previous three months. That resulted in quarterly revenue from the division of 2.10 billion Australian dollars, equivalent to US$1.48 billion.

Write to David Winning at david.winning@wsj.com

data.title: Zentek Issues Shareholder Letter Outlining Strategic Reset and Focused Growth Plan
data.description:
Company details next steps, priorities, and how capital will be allocated

Guelph, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - January 29, 2026) - Zentek Ltd. ("Zentek" or the "Company") today announced that it has issued a letter to shareholders outlining a strategic reset focused on capital discipline, sharper execution, and advancing a smaller number of core platforms with clear paths to value creation.

In the letter, Moe Jiwan, Zentek's Chief Executive Officer, outlines the Company's decision to narrow its focus to three core platforms: Albany Graphite, ZenGUARD, and Triera. Each will be managed against defined milestones, capital allocation criteria, and a 24-month roadmap. Other non-core initiatives have been paused or are being wound down to preserve capital and management focus.

The shareholder letter also details actions underway to reduce the Company's cost base and better align spending with near-term priorities. These actions include workforce and facility optimization, tighter controls on corporate spending, and a more disciplined approach to capital allocation. Future capital raises will be linked to specific milestones rather than open-ended development programs. As part of this reset, the Company announces the departure of its Chief Science Officer, Colin van der Kuur. The responsibilities of the Chief Science Officer role have been redistributed across the existing technical leadership team.

"This reset is about protecting and rebuilding shareholder value," said Moe Jiwan, Chief Executive Officer of Zentek Ltd. "We are confident we have the right technical expertise and operating team in place to execute against our priorities, advance our core platforms, and hold ourselves accountable to clear milestones and timelines."

The letter also outlines how each of the three core platforms will be advanced, how the Company is aligning its research and development efforts to support those priorities, the Company's approach to future financing, and new standards for transparency and reporting, including clearer segment-level financial disclosure and milestone tracking. The full shareholder letter is available on Zentek.com and on SEDAR+, and has been distributed directly to shareholders where an email address is available.

For further information, please contact:

Mohammed (Moe) Jiwan

647-287-9582

Mjiwan2@zentek.com

About Zentek Ltd.

Zentek Ltd. is a Canadian intellectual property development and commercialization company advancing a portfolio of graphene-enabled and advanced material technologies across healthcare, clean air and next-generation materials.

To find out more about Zentek Ltd., please visit our website at www.Zentek.com. A copy of this news release and all material documents in respect of the Company may be obtained on ZEN's SEDAR profile at www.sedarplus.ca.

Forward-Looking Statements

This news release contains forward-looking statements. Since forward-looking statements address future events and conditions, by their very nature they involve inherent risks and uncertainties. Although Zentek believes that the assumptions and factors used in preparing the forward-looking information in this news release are reasonable, undue reliance should not be placed on such information, which only applies as of the date of this news release, and no assurance can be given that such events will occur in the disclosed time frames or at all. Zentek disclaims any intention or obligation to update or revise any forward-looking information, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, other than as required by law.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/282110

data.title: Visa 1Q Sales Climb on Strong Holiday Shopping
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Visa logged higher revenue and profit in the first quarter, citing strong consumer spending through the holidays.

The payments company on Thursday posted a profit of $5.85 billion, or $3.03 a share, in the quarter ended Dec. 31, compared with $5.12 billion, or $2.58 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $3.17, ahead of the $3.14 anticipated by analysts, according to FactSet.

Revenue rose 15% to $10.90 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast revenue of $10.69 billion.

First-quarter performance was driven by resilient consumer spending and a strong holiday season, Visa Chief Executive Ryan McInerney said.

Payments volume increased 8%, cross-border volumes were up 12% and processed transactions rose 9%.

Visa said it repurchased 11 million shares of common stock at an average cost of $342.13 a share, totaling $3.8 billion, during the quarter.

The San Francisco company also deposited $500 million into its litigation escrow account, which was established to insulate the company from financial liability for certain litigation cases.

January has been a tough month for shares of both Visa and Mastercard after President Trump posted on social media his endorsement of the Credit Card Competition Act. The bill is aimed at reducing the credit-card swipe fees for retailers, which contribute significantly to Mastercard and Visa's earnings.

Earlier on Thursday, Mastercard reported an 18% jump in revenue and said consumer and business spending is healthy.

Mastercard also said it would cut 4% of its roughly 35,000 employees following a strategic review of its business.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Lockheed Martin Stock Jumps on Guidance and Government Missile Deal — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Lockheed Martin stock gained ground Thursday as management issued strong financial guidance for 2026 and disclosed a new missile deal with the Defense Department.

The defense contractor announced fourth-quarter earnings per share of $5.80 from sales of $20.3 billion. Wall Street was looking for earnings per share of $5.81 from sales of $19.9 billion.

For 2026, Lockheed expects earnings per share of between $29.35 and $30.25 from sales of about $78.8 billion. Wall Street currently projects earnings per share of about $29.50 from sales of $78 billion.

The company also said it has an agreement with the department "to quadruple the production of Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) interceptors, from 96 to 400 interceptors per year." Those are missiles intended to take out ballistic and hypersonic missiles in the upper atmosphere.

Lockheed shares rose 4.2% to $622.51, while the S&P 500 fell 0.1% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.1%. The rise left Lockheed's stock up 29% year to date.

The results and guidance were a relief to investors. Vertical Research Partners analyst Rob Stallard called the earnings report "in line," adding that revenue growth was good. He rates Lockheed stock at Hold and has a target of $530 for the price.

Coming into Wednesday trading, Lockheed's stock was up 23% year to date, outpacing the iShares Aerospace & Defense ETF by 13 percentage points. Still, that left shares 35 percentage points behind the ETF over the past 12 months.

"Lockheed Martin's share price underperformance...has been driven by perceived margin/portfolio risk despite clear upside drivers," wrote Jefferies analyst Sheila Kahyaoglu in a recent report.

A perceived risk is the F-35 program, which accounts for some 25% of Lockheed's overall business. The future of manned fighter jets has been the subject of debate for months amid a push for lower-cost drones. Manned fighters will be around for decades to come, but the issue has weighed on investor sentiment.

"Key [Lockheed] catalysts could include awards, program acceleration including [Patriot missiles], stabilizing margin performance...or portfolio realignment unlocking sum-of-the-parts value," said Kahyaoglu.

Increasing THAAD production qualifies as a positive factor, too.

L3Harris recently announced plans to spin off its missile business as a separately traded company, sparking ideas that other defense companies could follow suit. The idea is that higher-growth missile businesses should trade at higher multiples than large defense conglomerates.

Lockheed has some factors working in its favor, but Kahyaoglu still rates shares Hold and has a $540 price target for the stock.

There are challenges, too. High on the list is the threat of capital controls being imposed if projects fall behind schedule. President Donald Trump recently signed an executive order that could restrict defense contractors' ability to pay dividends and conduct share buybacks if the companies don't deliver innovative defense technologies on time and on budget. So far, that threat hasn't really hurt defense stocks.

The tailwinds and headwinds will be on the docket for discussion on Thursday and in the months to come.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Commercial Airspace Over Venezuela to Reopen, Trump Says — WSJ
data.description:
By Vera Bergengruen, Ryan Dubé and Kejal Vyas

President Trump told Venezuelan interim leader Delcy Rodriguez that he will be opening all commercial airspace over the country.

"American citizens will be very shortly able to go to Venezuela, and they'll be safe there," Trump said Thursday at a cabinet meeting at the White House.

Trump said he ordered Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy and U.S. military officials to work on opening up the airspace by the end of the day, lifting restrictions that have barred U.S. commercial aircraft from operating over Venezuela.

Commercial air travel between the U.S. and Venezuela has been suspended since 2019, when diplomatic relations between the two countries broke down. The State Department still lists Venezuela at the highest risk level for travel, warning U.S. citizens not to travel to or remain in the country due to high risk of wrongful detention, torture, terrorism, kidnapping and civil unrest.

After Trump's announcement, American Airlines said it would resume nonstop flights from the U.S. to Venezuela.

Trump on Thursday said the situation was improving and praised Rodriguez's leadership. "They have informed me they feel this very strong security," he said.

In November, Trump said the airspace surrounding Venezuela should be considered closed amid escalating tension and a military buildup that led to the Jan. 3 raid that captured Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro in Caracas.

The day of the operation, the Federal Aviation Administration banned U.S. commercial aircraft from operating in airspace above Venezuela and over that country's territorial waters.

Trump's announcement is the latest step in the administration's effort to normalize ties with Caracas. The State Department has formally notified Congress of plans to begin resuming operations in its U.S. Embassy in Caracas. On Thursday, Trump said major U.S. oil companies would travel to Venezuela to "scout out" locations for energy projects.

Rodriguez said this week her government was "unblocking" the country's frozen assets, which would be used to buy equipment for hospitals and for the electricity and gas sectors. On Thursday, Venezuela's congress was expected to approve changes to the hydrocarbons law that ease tight state control over its oil industry by providing private companies more autonomy to operate.

The reform will codify into law opaque oil deals known as productive participation contracts, which Maduro had implemented to circumvent U.S. sanctions on the oil industry. Companies will be able to sign contracts with state oil company Petróleos de Venezuela SA or its affiliates, allowing the private firms to operate state-owned oil fields.

The hydrocarbons bill keeps the maximum royalty payment that must be paid to the government, based on the volume of oil extracted, at 30%, but says the government can reduce the rate based on the economics of a project and other factors like capital investment requirements. U.S. and Venezuelan oil experts say the law will likely benefit companies already in Venezuela, but won't be enough to attract new foreign investments needed to revive the decrepit oil industry.

"It probably doesn't go far enough to attract sufficient investment, but it's a big step from where they were three weeks ago," Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Wednesday.

Since the operation that took down Maduro, the regime headed by Rodriguez, his former vice president, has largely maintained its repression tactics. Convoys of heavily armed and masked military intelligence officers patrol through the streets and set up roadblocks to inspect vehicles and often check passengers' phones.

Write to Vera Bergengruen at vera.bergengruen@wsj.com, Ryan Dubé at ryan.dube@wsj.com and Kejal Vyas at kejal.vyas@wsj.com

data.title: Deckers Outdoor Corp Bottom Line Advances In Q3
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Deckers Outdoor Corp (DECK) announced a profit for its third quarter that Increased, from last year

The company's bottom line totaled $481.14 million, or $3.33 per share. This compares with $456.73 million, or $3.00 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period rose 7.1% to $1.95 billion from $1.82 billion last year.

Deckers Outdoor Corp earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $481.14 Mln. vs. $456.73 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $3.33 vs. $3.00 last year.

-Revenue: $1.95 Bln vs. $1.82 Bln last year.

-Guidance:

Full year EPS guidance: $ 6.80 To $ 6.85

Full year revenue guidance: $ 5.400 B To $ 5.425 B

data.title: CME Group Raises Metals Margins
data.description:
CME GROUP INC:

  • RAISES COMEX COPPER FUTURES (0#HG:) MARGIN BY 20%

  • CME RAISES INITIAL MARGIN ON ITS COMEX 100 GOLD FUTURES (0#GC:) TO 6% FROM 5%

  • THE RATES WILL BE EFFECTIVE AFTER THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON JANUARY 30, 2026

data.title: Coeur d'Alene Bancorp Announces Annual Dividend
data.description:
COEUR D'ALENE, ID / ACCESS Newswire / January 29, 2026 / Coeur d'Alene Bancorp (CDAB), the parent company of bankcda, is pleased to announce a cash dividend of $.21 per share. The dividend will be payable on February 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 30, 2026.

Coeur d'Alene Bancorp, parent company of bankcda is headquartered in Coeur d'Alene Idaho with branches in Coeur d'Alene, Hayden, Kellogg, Post Falls, Spokane and Richland, WA.

For more information visit www.bankcda.com or contact Wes Veach or Mikayla Mahovsky at 208-665-5999.

SOURCE: Coeur d'Alene Bancorp

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Lighthouse Gold Announces Closing of Non-Brokered Private Placement of Units
data.description:
(TheNewswire)


Vancouver, B.C. – January 29,
2026 – TheNewswire -
Lighthouse Gold Inc. ("Company")
(CSE: LGHT) is pleased to
announce the closing of its non-brokered private placement offering
(the “Offering”), as previously disclosed in the Company’s news release
dated January 15, 2026 and January 16, 2026. In connection with the
Offering, the Company issued 12,200,000 units (the “Units”) at a price
of $0.07 per unit, generating aggregate gross proceeds of
approximately $854,000.


Each Unit will be comprised of one common share in the
capital of the Company (a “Share”) and one-half of one share purchase
warrant (each whole warrant, a “Warrant”). Each
Warrant is exercisable to purchase one additional Share (a
“Warrant Share”) for a period of 18 months from the closing date at an
exercise price of $0.13 per Warrant Share.


The Company intends to use the proceeds from the
Offering for exploration of its mineral properties and for general
working capital.


All securities issued in connection with the Offering
are subject to a statutory hold period of four months plus one day in
accordance with Canadian securities legislation.


A director of the Company participated in the Offering
being related parties as defined in Multilateral
Instrument 61-101 - Protection of Minority Security Holders in Special
Transactions ("MI 61- 101"),
participated in the Non-Brokered Offering. Any such related party
transaction was exempt from the formal valuation requirement and the
shareholder approval requirement of MI 61-101 as the fair market value
of any Common Shares issued to such persons did not exceed 25% of the
Company's market capitalization.


The securities referred to in this news release have
not been and will not be registered under the United States Securities
Act of 1933, as amended (the “U.S. Securities Act”) or any state
securities laws and may not be offered or sold within the United
States or to, or for the account or benefit of, U.S. persons absent
registration under the U.S. Securities Act and applicable state
securities laws, unless an exemption from such registration is
available. This news release does not constitute an offer for sale of
securities for sale, nor a solicitation for offers to buy any
securities. Any public offering of securities in the United States
must be made by means of a prospectus containing detailed information
about the Company and management, as well as financial statements.
“United States” and “U.S. person” have the respective meanings
assigned in Regulation S under the U.S Securities Act.


About Lighthouse Gold Inc.


Lighthouse Gold Inc. is a mineral exploration and
development company in the business of acquiring, and exploring gold
properties, with a focus in Guyana, South America. The company
currently has 100-per-cent interest in two properties located in
Guyana.


ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD OF
DIRECTORS


Geoff Balderson, Director, CFO & Corporate
Secretary


For more information, please
contact:


Geoff Balderson


Telephone: 604-602-0001


Email: gb@corporateminds.ca
